title: Source And Publication Refactor SSR-1 Inventory
summary: Freeze the current Docs Viewer source, payload, media, R2, route, and migration mapping before the source and publication cutover.
content_html:
Source And Publication Refactor SSR-1 Inventory

Checkpoint Result

SSR-1 is complete and no source, payload, media, public, external-local, or R2 byte has moved. This note is the disposable implementation inventory for the Source And Publication Model Refactor delivery.

The frozen cutover has these non-negotiable boundaries:

  • six configured scopes: five repository-backed scopes and one external-local Notes scope;
  • 477 canonical Markdown documents including this working note and the three Analysis Tags sub-scope documents;
  • configuration, not a literal folder name, becomes the topology authority;
  • repository and external-local Docs Viewer payload workspaces move from generated/ to published/;
  • public site/assets/data/ projections, R2 object keys, logical media identities, viewer URLs, and neutral management service routes do not follow that physical rename;
  • package-local Docs Review generated/ directories are a separate package format and do not move;
  • no R2 object is deleted until its exact replacement is present, byte-verified, rendered, and explicitly approved from this inventory.

Inventory snapshot:

  • scope config SHA-256: f3bc8df81ec3e0aca57e4f302dd599e634f8d822257177f1510af0a389d9d9c7;
  • R2 docs/ listing: 135 objects, 13 prefix markers, 122 media objects, 20,113,227 media bytes;
  • R2 listing snapshot SHA-256: 89c0555c63dc829caa27d91e635b2108f391feac635c30126cacf1d5274a5586;
  • no repository or configured external-local duplicate was found for the 11 Library/Analysis R2 media objects.

The source and payload manifest hashes below are SHA-256 hashes of sorted relative path|size|file SHA-256 lines. They let later migration preflight reject a stale plan without embedding 477 tracked filenames here. The Studio source aggregate excludes this self-describing note; the note is counted separately and SSR-3 must include its then-current hash in the migration preflight.

Current Support Matrix And Blockers

role repository external-local R2 current blocker or branch
Canonical document source Supported through filesystem paths. Supported through expanded absolute paths and containment checks. Not supported. Source model, mutation, watcher, and lifecycle require enumerable writable filesystem paths.
Docs/search payload workspace Supported under hard-coded docs-viewer/generated expectations. Supported under hard-coded external generated expectations. Not supported. Builders and readers accept paths, but validators, lifecycle planners, manifests, routes, and browser config encode the old topology.
Public document/search projection Supported only under site/assets/data. Not supported. Not supported. publish_output validation and the publish gate require tracked public paths.
Published img or files media repo_assets works only for non-public repo-backed scopes. external_assets works only inside the external source scope. r2_upload works only for public read-only scopes at docs/<scope>/<type>. storage_mode branches in config normalization, import planning/apply, package media writes, media serving, and upload preflight.
Published HTML One special repository path and interactive-html token. No typed contract. No typed contract. HTML behavior and its physical interactive/ folder are coupled.
Remote inventory/read/delete No common role/location contract. No common role/location contract. Upload code supports HEAD/PUT and an underlying DELETE, but Docs workflows do not expose list/read/stage/capabilities. SSR-2 must add the shared adapter boundary before orchestration changes.

Current media validation also permits only img and files, ties public R2 use to media_path_prefix == docs/<scope>, rejects external-local repository targets, and treats public projection status as a storage-mode decision. These are implementation restrictions, not target policy.

Literal generated/ Dependency Inventory

Must change with the workspace cutover

  • Configuration and validation: docs-viewer/config/scopes/docs_scopes.json, docs-viewer/config/scopes/docs_scope_manifest.json, docs-viewer/services/docs_scope_config.py, and docs-viewer/services/docs_publish_gate.py.
  • Builder and browser projection: docs-viewer/build/docs_builder/browser_config.py, source.py, reference_artifacts.py, semantic_target_lookup.py, docs-viewer/config/semantic-references/registry.json, the private browser defaults, and the manage route config.
  • Read and serving boundary: docs-viewer/services/docs_generated_reads.py, docs_viewer_service.py, docs_management_read_service.py, and the static /docs-viewer/generated/ allowlist.
  • Scope lifecycle and manifest: docs_scope_manifest.py, docs_scope_create.py, docs_scope_rename.py, docs_scope_delete.py, docs_sub_scope_lifecycle.py, docs_scope_manifest.py, and docs-viewer/runtime/js/management/docs-viewer-scope-lifecycle.js.
  • Generated browser consumers: site/docs-viewer/runtime/js/shared/docs-viewer-data.js and docs-viewer-generated-data-runtime.js.
  • Semantic references: the target lookup path and the Moments input paths move to configured published/ locations.
  • Tests and fixtures: generated_output_contracts.json, repo_factory.py, the build/import/management test-support modules, and the focused build, search, source, lifecycle, import, generated-read, publish, export, media, semantic-reference, service-smoke, and public-static checks returned by the SSR-1 scan.

The exact focused test files are:

test_build_docs_cli.py, test_build_docs_external_scopes.py, test_build_docs_payloads.py, test_build_docs_public_payloads.py, test_build_docs_subscopes.py, test_build_search_python.py, test_docs_broken_links.py, test_docs_data_sharing_source_context.py, test_docs_export.py, test_docs_generated_reads.py, test_docs_import.py, test_docs_import_review_sources.py, test_docs_management_capabilities.py, test_docs_management_metadata.py, test_docs_media_storage.py, test_docs_publish_gate.py, test_docs_scope_config.py, test_docs_scope_lifecycle.py, test_docs_static_html_export.py, test_docs_subtree_copy.py, test_docs_viewer_static_assets.py, test_docs_write_rebuild.py, test_semantic_target_lookup.py, docs_viewer_semantic_token_picker_modules.py, docs_viewer_service_manage.py, and docs_viewer_service_review.py.

Must remain separate

  • Public site/assets/data/ projection paths remain unchanged.
  • The neutral service routes /docs/index-tree, /docs/recent, /docs/doc, /docs/search, /docs/references, and /docs/reference-target remain stable.
  • Existing /docs/generated/* alternatives are compatibility aliases. Active browser consumers currently call them even though the neutral routes already exist. SSR-2 retargets those consumers and removes the *_ALT_PATH constants, dispatch branches, and fixtures; no alias survives the cutover.
  • Docs Review sessions and validated packages use a package-internal generated/ directory in docs_review_packages.py, docs_review_sessions.py, docs_review_materialization.py, and their package-format documentation. That is not the repository/external Docs Viewer workspace and remains unchanged.
  • A data-sharing payload field named generated is provenance data and remains unchanged.

Documentation consequences at closeout

Current-path statements require later updates in Source Organisation, Source Tree Ownership, Scope Lifecycle, Docs Viewer Builder, Docs Build Watcher, Current File Structure, Runtime, the generated-artifact reference, Recent, Embedded Detail Documents, semantic references, and the Studio code authority map. Feature and delivery documents that describe the migration remain historical context and are not rewritten as current-state authorities.

Frozen docs_scopes_v2 Shape

SSR-2 replaces source, output, search_output, publish_output, publish_search_output, media_path_prefix, import_media_storage, and external_data_root with role-first objects. Presentation and policy fields such as scope_id, scope_type, route settings, document defaults, viewability lists, and status settings remain.

{
  "schema_version": "docs_scopes_v2",
  "scopes": [
    {
      "scope_id": "studio",
      "scope_type": "local",
      "source": {
        "location": {
          "provider": "repository",
          "path": "docs-viewer/source/studio"
        },
        "documents_path": "documents",
        "build_media": {},
        "sub_scopes_path": "sub-scopes"
      },
      "published": {
        "documents": {
          "location": {
            "provider": "repository",
            "path": "docs-viewer/published/docs/studio"
          }
        },
        "search": {
          "location": {
            "provider": "repository",
            "path": "docs-viewer/published/search/studio/index.json"
          }
        },
        "media": {
          "img": {
            "reference_prefix": "docs/studio/img",
            "location": {
              "provider": "repository",
              "path": "docs-viewer/published/docs/studio/media/img"
            },
            "served_path_prefix": "/docs/media/studio/img",
            "build_inputs": {}
          }
        }
      },
      "public_projection": null,
      "sub_scopes": []
    }
  ]
}

Schema rules:

  • location.provider is exactly repository, external_local, or r2; location.path is a confined filesystem path or object-key prefix appropriate to that provider.
  • source.location.path is the owned scope container. documents_path, build_media[*].path, and sub_scopes_path are confined relative paths beneath it.
  • Each build_media entry names path, producer, and publishes_to; no entry is added until a real build-source type exists. A later Mermaid entry can publish to the one img target without moving ready images.
  • published.documents and published.search are the Docs Viewer local consumer-ready payload locations. Public copies do not overload them.
  • Every configured published media type has one reference_prefix, one location, one served_path_prefix, and zero or more explicit build_inputs. There is no scope-wide media storage mode.
  • public_projection is either null or contains independent documents and search repository locations. It does not select the media provider.
  • A sub-scope repeats the same source, published, and optional public_projection objects beneath its own configured roots.
  • External-local policy requires every source and media location to use external_local and remain beneath the configured external scope folder; its document/search payload workspace remains beneath the external Docs Viewer root.
  • Browser-safe projection exposes viewer settings, logical URLs, and served paths only. It never exposes source paths, filesystem locations, credentials, or write targets.

Frozen Path And Media Mapping

scope source documents published documents/search public projection media policy
Studio source/studio/documents published/docs/studio; published/search/studio/index.json none img and files beneath published/docs/studio/media; local /docs/media/studio/<type> serving.
Library source/library/documents published/docs/library; published/search/library/index.json existing Library docs/search site paths img and html under site/assets/data/docs/scopes/library/media/<type>; files at R2 docs/library/files.
Analysis source/analysis/documents published/docs/analysis; published/search/analysis/index.json existing Analysis docs/search site paths img under site/assets/data/docs/scopes/analysis/media/img; files at R2 docs/analysis/files.
Analysis Tags source/analysis/sub-scopes/tags/documents published/docs/analysis/sub-scopes/tags existing site/assets/data/docs/scopes/analysis/tags no media type currently registered.
Moments source/moments/documents published/docs/moments; published/search/moments/index.json existing Moments docs/search site paths img and files retain R2 docs/moments/<type>.
Processing source/processing/documents published/docs/processing; published/search/processing/index.json none current img and files move to published/docs/processing/media/<type>; local /docs/media/processing/<type> serving.
Notes external source/notes/documents external published/docs/notes; published/search/notes/index.json none all media stays beneath external source/notes/media/<type>; local /docs/media/notes/<type> serving.

Logical Markdown media identities such as docs/analysis/img/name.webp and current /docs/media/processing/... references do not change. Repository, external-local, and R2 adapters resolve those identities to their configured served path. Public document payload URLs remain under /assets/data/; repository management payload URLs change from /docs-viewer/generated/ to /docs-viewer/published/; external-local management reads use the neutral /docs/* service routes.

Media Inventory And Disposition

R2 summary

scope objects bytes source-reference result disposition
Analysis 10 711,036 9 active exact matches; image 02 appears only inside a Markdown comment keep the PDF in R2; migrate all nine clearly attributable images to the repository image root, including inactive image 02, then delete only those nine image objects after verification and approval.
Library 1 84,493 1 active exact match migrate to the repository image root, then delete the one R2 image after verification and approval.
Moments 111 19,317,698 36 active -800 references; 72 companion variants; one unreferenced three-size family retain all objects in R2; the unreferenced family is scope-owned but not a cleanup target in this refactor.

The 13 zero-byte prefix objects are docs/, scope prefixes for Analysis, Library, Moments, and Studio, and their existing img/ or files/ markers. They remain provider scaffolding and are not part of the media deletion list.

Current uploader comparison treats a non-multipart ETag as MD5. SSR-5 must still download/stage each migrated object and recompute MD5 before any delete.

Repository and external-local media

  • Processing owns 40 ready files objects at docs-viewer/source/processing/media/files, totalling 4,077,435 bytes. Their sorted relative path|size|file SHA-256 manifest SHA-256 is 11177efc8a3b871643e6509ad210677b4eccf5f6792a8851851c93738a47ed5a. Seventeen are actively referenced and 23 are unreferenced but still scope-owned. All move byte-for-byte to docs-viewer/published/docs/processing/media/files; none is deleted.
  • Studio, Analysis, and Notes have materialized media/img and media/files directories but no ready media bytes in them.
  • site/assets/docs/interactive/library/coincidence-salience-demo.html is 3,064 bytes with MD5 68d6671ae6f675408c81d35a4065c1fd. It is actively referenced by interactive-html and moves once to site/assets/data/docs/scopes/library/media/html/coincidence-salience-demo.html; the old copy is removed only after the new sandboxed handler passes.

The Processing file set is frozen by its repository bytes. Migration preflight must re-enumerate exactly 40 files, 4,077,435 bytes, and reject any changed manifest before moving them.

Migration Order And Recovery Boundary

  1. SSR-2 introduces docs_scopes_v2 and repository, external-local, and R2 location adapters while expressing every current path first. No byte moves during that compatibility-free model transition.
  2. Main build, import, inventory, publish, serving, and lifecycle workflows switch to role plus required capabilities. Storage-mode branches and literal generated-root validators are removed.
  3. Browser consumers switch to neutral /docs/* routes; /docs/generated/* aliases are removed. Static repository payload URLs are regenerated from config.
  4. With watchers and writers quiesced, preflight the source, payload, public, local-media, external-local, and R2 manifest hashes. Any mismatch stops before writes and requires a fresh SSR-1 inventory decision.
  5. Rename repository and external payload workspaces to published/, update config in the same bounded cutover, rebuild, and compare output routes and bytes. Public projections and R2 remain untouched.
  6. SSR-3 moves Markdown to documents/ and Analysis Tags to sub-scopes/tags/documents/ using a complete one-to-one manifest. No fallback read or old-path alias is added.
  7. SSR-4 moves ready local media and the Library HTML artifact to their one published roots, then verifies serving before removing old local copies.
  8. SSR-5 downloads the ten Library/Analysis R2 images selected above to temporary staging, recomputes MD5 and size, atomically places them at tracked targets, flips only the affected img locations, rebuilds public payloads, and verifies rendered URLs.
  9. Remote deletion is a separate final action. It is limited to the exact ten image keys above, requires explicit approval after replacement verification, performs a final HEAD/MD5 comparison, and never touches the Analysis PDF, Moments objects, or prefix markers.

Local phases fail closed before mutation when paths collide, manifests drift, adapters lack capabilities, or containment fails. After a local mutation begins, recovery is the exact inverse move plus the paired config restoration; no hidden dual-read period exists. A public projection is not updated until its local build is complete. If an R2 delete partially fails, the repository replacement remains authoritative and the undeleted remote objects are reported for retry; source and config are not rolled back to R2.

SSR-2 Entry Conditions

  • Implement only the frozen schema and location boundary first.
  • Preserve current bytes, logical references, public paths, and neutral routes while replacing provider branches.
  • Remove the discovered /docs/generated/* compatibility aliases as part of the route cutover.
  • Stop before SSR-3 document moves and request checkpoint review after SSR-2 evidence passes.
